changes.value:
## What

Bug: Editing the board name in vim mode doesn't visibly update the inspector or window title bar.

### Root cause analysis

Two issues converge:

**1. `saveInPlace` incorrectly closes the editor**

`TextEditor.saveInPlace()` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx:194`) calls `onCommitRef.current(text)`. In `Field.tsx`, this routes to `handleCommit` (line 130), which calls `updateField()` (async, fire-and-forget) + `onDone?.()` (synchronous — closes the editor).

But `saveInPlace` is designed for vim insert→normal transitions — it should save WITHOUT closing. The fix: Field.tsx needs a separate `onSaveInPlace` callback (or the existing `onChange`/autosave path) that calls `updateField` without calling `onDone`.

**2. Window title and inspector must react to the save**

After `updateField` fires:
- Rust `dispatch_command` runs → `flush_and_emit_for_handle` → `board_display_name` → `update_window_title` (sets native window title)
- Frontend receives "board-changed" Tauri event → entity store refreshes → `useFieldValue("board", ..., displayFieldName)` in `board-selector.tsx:60` re-renders

Verify this chain actually fires end-to-end for the board name field. The board-selector (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx:77`) renders `<Field entityType="board" entityId={boardEntity.id}>` — check that the entity store subscription works for the board entity type.

### Files involved
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx` — `saveInPlace` (line 194) calls `onCommit` which closes editor
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` — `handleCommit` (line 130) always calls `onDone()`
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx` — `useFieldValue` for live board name (line 60)
- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — `update_window_title` (line 49), `board_display_name` (line 64), title refresh after dispatch (line 1260)

### Approach

Option A (minimal): Make `saveInPlace` use the debounced `onChange` path instead of `onCommit`. Since `onChange` goes through `useDebouncedSave` → `updateField` WITHOUT calling `onDone`, the editor stays open and the save still fires (after debounce delay). The vim insert→normal transition would trigger an immediate save via `onChange` instead of `onCommit`.

Option B (more correct): Add a dedicated `onSaveInPlace` prop to `FieldEditorProps` that calls `updateField` without `onDone`. Pass it from Field.tsx to editors. TextEditor's `saveInPlace` calls `onSaveInPlace` instead of `onCommit`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] In vim mode: pressing Escape in insert mode saves the board name but keeps the editor open in normal mode
- [ ] After save, the window title bar updates to reflect the new board name
- [ ] After save, the board-selector shows the updated name
- [ ] After save, if the board entity is inspected, the inspector shows the updated name
- [ ] Existing commit-on-exit behavior (blur, Enter) still works and closes the editor

## Tests
- [ ] Update `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/editor-save.test.tsx` — add vim saveInPlace test: verify save fires without onDone
- [ ] Manual test: edit board name in vim mode, press Escape from insert mode, verify title bar and board-selector update
- [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app test` passes
Board name edit in vim mode: inspector and window title don't update

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,45 +1,41 @@
 ## What

-Bug: Editing the board name in vim mode doesn't visibly update the inspector or window title bar.
+Bug: Editing the board name — the debounced autosave fires but the window title bar and inspector don't visually update until the editor is closed. The user expects to see the title and inspector reflect changes as they type (after the 1s debounce).

-### Root cause analysis
+Save-on-exit works fine. The issue is that the autosave path doesn't trigger the same UI refresh cascade.

-Two issues converge:
+### Diagnosis chain

-**1. `saveInPlace` incorrectly closes the editor**
+The autosave fires: `TextEditor onChange` → `Field.debouncedOnChange` → `useDebouncedSave` (1s) → `updateField` → `backendDispatch("entity.update_field")` → Rust `dispatch_command`.

-`TextEditor.saveInPlace()` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx:194`) calls `onCommitRef.current(text)`. In `Field.tsx`, this routes to `handleCommit` (line 130), which calls `updateField()` (async, fire-and-forget) + `onDone?.()` (synchronous — closes the editor).
+After the Rust command completes:
+1. **Window title**: `flush_and_emit_for_handle` → `board_display_name` → `update_window_title` (`commands.rs:1260-1271`)
+2. **Frontend**: `flush_and_emit` emits "board-changed" Tauri event → entity store refreshes → `useFieldValue("board", ..., displayFieldName)` in `board-selector.tsx:60` re-renders

-But `saveInPlace` is designed for vim insert→normal transitions — it should save WITHOUT closing. The fix: Field.tsx needs a separate `onSaveInPlace` callback (or the existing `onChange`/autosave path) that calls `updateField` without calling `onDone`.
+Investigate why one or both of these don't trigger during autosave but do trigger on commit-on-exit. Possible causes:
+- The autosave `updateField` call is the same as commit's — both go through `backendDispatch`. So the Rust side should behave identically. Check if it does.
+- The entity store refresh might be suppressed while the editor is open (e.g. the Field component's `useFieldValue` returns the stale value because it's in editing mode)
+- The window title update in Rust might not fire for some reason on the autosave path

-**2. Window title and inspector must react to the save**
-
-After `updateField` fires:
-- Rust `dispatch_command` runs → `flush_and_emit_for_handle` → `board_display_name` → `update_window_title` (sets native window title)
-- Frontend receives "board-changed" Tauri event → entity store refreshes → `useFieldValue("board", ..., displayFieldName)` in `board-selector.tsx:60` re-renders
+### Files to investigate
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/use-debounced-save.ts` — verify the debounced save actually fires (add logging)
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/field-update-context.tsx` — `updateField` is the same for both paths
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx:60` — `useFieldValue` subscription for board name
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-store-context.tsx` — does the entity store refresh when "board-changed" fires during editing?
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1260` — window title refresh after dispatch

-Verify this chain actually fires end-to-end for the board name field. The board-selector (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx:77`) renders `<Field entityType="board" entityId={boardEntity.id}>` — check that the entity store subscription works for the board entity type.
-
-### Files involved
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx` — `saveInPlace` (line 194) calls `onCommit` which closes editor
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` — `handleCommit` (line 130) always calls `onDone()`
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx` — `useFieldValue` for live board name (line 60)
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — `update_window_title` (line 49), `board_display_name` (line 64), title refresh after dispatch (line 1260)
-
 ### Approach
+1. Add `console.warn` instrumentation to confirm the debounced save fires
+2. Check the macOS unified log (`log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'`) for the Rust-side dispatch
+3. Trace whether "board-changed" event is emitted and whether the entity store processes it
+4. Fix whatever link in the chain is broken

-Option A (minimal): Make `saveInPlace` use the debounced `onChange` path instead of `onCommit`. Since `onChange` goes through `useDebouncedSave` → `updateField` WITHOUT calling `onDone`, the editor stays open and the save still fires (after debounce delay). The vim insert→normal transition would trigger an immediate save via `onChange` instead of `onCommit`.
-
-Option B (more correct): Add a dedicated `onSaveInPlace` prop to `FieldEditorProps` that calls `updateField` without `onDone`. Pass it from Field.tsx to editors. TextEditor's `saveInPlace` calls `onSaveInPlace` instead of `onCommit`.
-
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] In vim mode: pressing Escape in insert mode saves the board name but keeps the editor open in normal mode
-- [ ] After save, the window title bar updates to reflect the new board name
-- [ ] After save, the board-selector shows the updated name
-- [ ] After save, if the board entity is inspected, the inspector shows the updated name
-- [ ] Existing commit-on-exit behavior (blur, Enter) still works and closes the editor
+- [ ] While typing the board name (after 1s debounce), the window title bar updates to reflect the new name
+- [ ] While typing the board name (after 1s debounce), the board-selector shows the updated name
+- [ ] While typing the board name (after 1s debounce), if the inspector is open on the board entity, it shows the updated name
+- [ ] Commit-on-exit behavior unchanged

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Update `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/editor-save.test.tsx` — add vim saveInPlace test: verify save fires without onDone
-- [ ] Manual test: edit board name in vim mode, press Escape from insert mode, verify title bar and board-selector update
+- [ ] Manual test: edit board name, wait 2s (longer than debounce), verify title bar updates without closing editor
 - [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app test` passes
\ No newline at end of file

--- original

 ## What

-Bug: Editing the board name — the debounced autosave fires but the window title bar and inspector don't visually update until the editor is closed. The user expects to see the title and inspector reflect changes as they type (after the 1s debounce).
+Bug: Editing the board name in vim mode doesn't visibly update the inspector or window title bar.
+
+### Root cause analysis
+
+Two issues converge:
+
+**1. `saveInPlace` incorrectly closes the editor**

-Save-on-exit works fine. The issue is that the autosave path doesn't trigger the same UI refresh cascade.
+`TextEditor.saveInPlace()` (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx:194`) calls `onCommitRef.current(text)`. In `Field.tsx`, this routes to `handleCommit` (line 130), which calls `updateField()` (async, fire-and-forget) + `onDone?.()` (synchronous — closes the editor).

-### Diagnosis chain
+But `saveInPlace` is designed for vim insert→normal transitions — it should save WITHOUT closing. The fix: Field.tsx needs a separate `onSaveInPlace` callback (or the existing `onChange`/autosave path) that calls `updateField` without calling `onDone`.

-The autosave fires: `TextEditor onChange` → `Field.debouncedOnChange` → `useDebouncedSave` (1s) → `updateField` → `backendDispatch("entity.update_field")` → Rust `dispatch_command`.
+**2. Window title and inspector must react to the save**

-After the Rust command completes:
-1. **Window title**: `flush_and_emit_for_handle` → `board_display_name` → `update_window_title` (`commands.rs:1260-1271`)
-2. **Frontend**: `flush_and_emit` emits "board-changed" Tauri event → entity store refreshes → `useFieldValue("board", ..., displayFieldName)` in `board-selector.tsx:60` re-renders
+After `updateField` fires:
+- Rust `dispatch_command` runs → `flush_and_emit_for_handle` → `board_display_name` → `update_window_title` (sets native window title)
+- Frontend receives "board-changed" Tauri event → entity store refreshes → `useFieldValue("board", ..., displayFieldName)` in `board-selector.tsx:60` re-renders

-Investigate why one or both of these don't trigger during autosave but do trigger on commit-on-exit. Possible causes:
-- The autosave `updateField` call is the same as commit's — both go through `backendDispatch`. So the Rust side should behave identically. Check if it does.
-- The entity store refresh might be suppressed while the editor is open (e.g. the Field component's `useFieldValue` returns the stale value because it's in editing mode)
-- The window title update in Rust might not fire for some reason on the autosave path
+Verify this chain actually fires end-to-end for the board name field. The board-selector (`kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx:77`) renders `<Field entityType="board" entityId={boardEntity.id}>` — check that the entity store subscription works for the board entity type.

-### Files to investigate
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/use-debounced-save.ts` — verify the debounced save actually fires (add logging)
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/field-update-context.tsx` — `updateField` is the same for both paths
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx:60` — `useFieldValue` subscription for board name
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/lib/entity-store-context.tsx` — does the entity store refresh when "board-changed" fires during editing?
-- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs:1260` — window title refresh after dispatch
+### Files involved
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/text-editor.tsx` — `saveInPlace` (line 194) calls `onCommit` which closes editor
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/field.tsx` — `handleCommit` (line 130) always calls `onDone()`
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-selector.tsx` — `useFieldValue` for live board name (line 60)
+- `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` — `update_window_title` (line 49), `board_display_name` (line 64), title refresh after dispatch (line 1260)

 ### Approach
-1. Add `console.warn` instrumentation to confirm the debounced save fires
-2. Check the macOS unified log (`log show --predicate 'subsystem == "com.swissarmyhammer.kanban"'`) for the Rust-side dispatch
-3. Trace whether "board-changed" event is emitted and whether the entity store processes it
-4. Fix whatever link in the chain is broken

+Option A (minimal): Make `saveInPlace` use the debounced `onChange` path instead of `onCommit`. Since `onChange` goes through `useDebouncedSave` → `updateField` WITHOUT calling `onDone`, the editor stays open and the save still fires (after debounce delay). The vim insert→normal transition would trigger an immediate save via `onChange` instead of `onCommit`.
+
+Option B (more correct): Add a dedicated `onSaveInPlace` prop to `FieldEditorProps` that calls `updateField` without `onDone`. Pass it from Field.tsx to editors. TextEditor's `saveInPlace` calls `onSaveInPlace` instead of `onCommit`.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] While typing the board name (after 1s debounce), the window title bar updates to reflect the new name
-- [ ] While typing the board name (after 1s debounce), the board-selector shows the updated name
-- [ ] While typing the board name (after 1s debounce), if the inspector is open on the board entity, it shows the updated name
-- [ ] Commit-on-exit behavior unchanged
+- [ ] In vim mode: pressing Escape in insert mode saves the board name but keeps the editor open in normal mode
+- [ ] After save, the window title bar updates to reflect the new board name
+- [ ] After save, the board-selector shows the updated name
+- [ ] After save, if the board entity is inspected, the inspector shows the updated name
+- [ ] Existing commit-on-exit behavior (blur, Enter) still works and closes the editor

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Manual test: edit board name, wait 2s (longer than debounce), verify title bar updates without closing editor
+- [ ] Update `kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/editor-save.test.tsx` — add vim saveInPlace test: verify save fires without onDone
+- [ ] Manual test: edit board name in vim mode, press Escape from insert mode, verify title bar and board-selector update
 - [ ] `pnpm --filter kanban-app test` passes
